info.name: Cookies without HttpOnly attribute - Detect
author: rj coleman
mr.bobo hp
info.description: Checks whether cookies in the HTTP response contain the HttpOnly attribute. If the HttpOnly flag is set, it means that the cookie is HTTP-only
info.impact: Lack of HttpOnly flag on cookies increases the risk of client-side attacks, compromising user session security and data privacy.
info.remediation: Ensure that all cookies are set with the HttpOnly attribute to prevent client-side access.

info.name: Cookies without Secure attribute - Detect
author: vthiery
rj coleman
info.description: Checks whether cookies in the HTTP response contain the Secure attribute. If the Secure flag is set, it means that the cookie can only be transmitted over HTTPS
info.impact: Lack of Secure flag on cookies allows the cookie to be sent over unsecure HTTP, making it vulnerable to man-in-the-middle (MITM) attacks.
info.remediation: Ensure that all cookies are set with the Secure attribute to prevent MITM attacks.

info.name: WAF Detection
author: dwisiswant0
lu4nx
info.description: A web application firewall was detected.

request:
POST /dvwa/vulnerabilities/sqli/ HTTP/1.1
Host: 10.97.31.123
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Kubuntu; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/135.0.0.0 Safari/537.36
Connection: close
Content-Length: 27
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Accept-Encoding: gzip

_=<script>alert(1)</script>
response:
HTTP/1.1 302 Found Connection: close Transfer-Encoding: chunked Cache-Control: no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 Date: Thu, 05 Mar 2026 08:06:51 GMT Expires: Thu, 19 Nov 1981 08:52:00 GMT Location: ../../login.php Pragma: no-cache Server: Apache/2.4.23 (Win32) OpenSSL/1.0.2j mod_fcgid/2.3.9 Set-Cookie: security=impossible; path=/; HttpOnly Set-Cookie: PHPSESSID=klknkdst3c6eae8mhum1ck72f2; path=/ X-Powered-By: PHP/7.0.12
Notice: Array to string conversion in C:\phpStudy\PHPTutorial\WWW\DVWA\dvwa\includes\dvwaPage.inc.php on line 78
curl-command: curl -X 'POST' -d '_=<script>alert(1)</script>' -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' -H 'Host: 10.97.31.123' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Kubuntu; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/135.0.0.0 Safari/537.36' 'http://10.97.31.123/dvwa/vulnerabilities/sqli/'
